observations.recommendation:
1.16. We reiterated and made additional recommendation and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) sustain the timely preparation of bank
reconciliation statements; and (ii) on identified/segregated irreconcilable difference
in the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the GF, exert effort to reduce the unreconcilable
balance;
(b) the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct
reconciliation of books of accounts at least quarterly to prevent further
accumulation/occurrence of discrepancies in their respective records in the future;
and
(c) the Municipal Treasurer to check the reconciling items in the treasury’s books
and, if warranted, effect the necessary adjusting entry to correct the noted
misstatements.

2.27. We restated our prior years' recommendation and supplement additions
thereto that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
(a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered
items as called for in the above-quoted regulation and value inventories using moving
average method; (ii) ensure complete documentation in reference to COA Circular
Nos. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023; (iii)
immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct inventory
balance upon receipt of the SSMI from the GSO, as basis for the recording of expenses
as prescribed above; and (iv) examine noted deviations in item 1.23 of this
Memorandum, for future similar transactions and adhere closely with the
requirements of the law prior payment thereof; and
(b) the GSO to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for all category of purchased
supplies and materials; (ii) consolidate all purchase requests pertaining to the
procurement of supplies and submit the consolidated request to the Bids and Awards
Committee for centralized procurement to avail of the quantity discount inherent to
bulk orders and procure, as far as practicable, directly from the PS-DBM all
anticipated common-use supplies and equipment, and resort to local suppliers only
upon unavailability of supplies needed; (iii) see to it that proof of unavailability in the
local depot is attached to procurements coursed through suppliers other than the PS-
DBM; and (iv) in coordination with implementing departments to henceforth, timely
submit the RIS or other proof of distribution/utilization and SSMI to the Municipal
Accountant for proper recording; and
(c) the Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count of inventories,
to detect discrepancies and to safeguard the inventories of the Municipality.

3.43. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and supplemented additions
thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) work coordinately with the Municipal
Engineer on the inventory of all local road projects with a complete description and cost
segregation of road components to ensure proper recording and control of the
assets; (ii) undertake the reclassification of the assets that meet the recognition
criteria of semi-expendable properties; (iii) review and verify the noted errors in
recording of capitalizable expenses for CY 2024 and prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to correct the misstatements of the affected
accounts; (iv) henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation,
improvement, and construction of infrastructure assets are recognized as PPE, in
accordance with IPSAS No. 45, and strictly observe the capitalization threshold
provided under COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; (v) recognize
depreciation using lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each
component at the end of the year and asset impairment to reflect the accurate value
of PPE in the financial statements; (vi) reclassify the noted completed projects for CY
2023 to 2024 that remain recorded as CIP; (vii) investigate the status of the projects
from the CY 2018 to CY 2024 that still recorded as CIP account and effect the
necessary entries based from the results of the investigation; and (viii) prepare the
respective PPE Ledger Cards not only for the usual accounts but also for Local Road
Network as required by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2015-
008 dated November 25, 2015; and
(b) require the General Services Office to exert efforts to prepare the Property
Cards, Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Card, starting
with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly
increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered.

4.22. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) set-up RPT and SET receivables and deferred
property tax at the beginning of the year based on duly certified list of the taxpayers
and the amount due and collectible for the year and year thereafter provided by the
Municipal Treasurer parallel with the requirement of the MNGAS; (ii) recognize
appropriate discounts and penalties based on Abstract of Real Property Tax
cognizant with International standards of accounting provided above; and (iii)
henceforth observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET
receivable and deferred income accounts; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) in coordination with the Municipal Assessor,
submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly certified list of
the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and years thereafter as
reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables and deferred accounts at the beginning
of the year by setting a target of a reasonable percentage annually until all properties
and RPT taxpayers are covered; and (ii) continue with the collection strategy being
employed as at the moment and stringent information dissemination especially at the
time of grant of tax amnesty. Otherwise, after the lapse of the amnesty recommend
imposition of civil remedies, if warranted as provided under Section 174 of the LGC
to encourage the taxpayers to pay their tax on time

5.23. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive:
(a) indulge the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the approval of the adjustment of
tax rates of the current Local Revenue Code, to increase the revenue generation of
the LGU; and
(b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) hire additional personnel to exercise
the power to examine the books of accounts of businesses, especially to those
taxpayers with doubtful gross sales/tax declaration to ascertain the correctness of tax
to be collected; and (ii) submit the Stratified Schedule of Presumptive Income Level
to be incorporated in the proposed revision of the Local Tax Code of the LGU, to
serve as a legal basis in the tax assessment of those taxpayers who cannot provide
gross sales.

6.41. We reiterated and supplemented our previous year’s recommendations that
the Local Chief Executive to require the BAC, BAC Secretariat and BAC-TWG:
(a) in coordination with the former BAC Chairman and Members, if warranted
submit the lacking documents of the projects mentioned in items 7.15 and 7.19 of this
Report, for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action;
(b) paying attention to the other deficiencies cited in Annex B of this Report, to
avoid the recurrence thereof in future procurements;
(c) require end-users/implementing department/s to prepare PPMPs as the basis
for the preparation of the indicative APP, to be used during budget preparation as
compliance with the budget-plan linkage of all procurements;
(d) invite observers coming from the necessitated organization as provided above;
(e) necessitate the posting on the PhilGEPS website the NTP and within the
reglementary period provided under the RIRR;
(f) furnishing the COA with copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers
relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract and sending of
notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor
pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006;
(g) for future similar undertakings on infrastructure projects, (i) conducting
complete detailed engineering prior to the preparation of bid documents in order to
assure the viability of infrastructure projects; and (iii) conducting materials and
items of work testing by the Municipal Engineer or the Project Engineer.
6.42. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously instruct the
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the required supporting
documents for every transaction before any payment is made.

7.34. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to ensure that:
(a) except for cash advances for training and travel, all special disbursing officers
are formally designated and adequately bonded prior to the grant of the initial cash
advance;
(b) the Municipal Accountant (i) checks the completeness of the supporting
documents on cash advances and liquidation reports submitted; (ii) consistently issues
certification that the previous cash advance is liquidated/accounted for before the
grant of another cash advance; and (iii) strictly monitors timely submission of
liquidation reports and immediate refund of any unused balances and the timely
accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances;
(c) AOs comply with the required number of days within which to liquidate cash
advances granted and the necessary supporting documents are attached to the
liquidation reports;
(d) cash advances granted to Special Disbursing Officers are within the limit of
their cash accountability;
(e) travelling expenses are given pursuant to EO 77 and lecturers/resource
persons are paid in accordance with Budget Circular 2007-1; and
(f) granting of CAs is limited to disbursements allowed by law and adheres closely
to the requirements of the procurement law or RA 9184 and resorts to cash advance only
on meritorious cases.

8.18. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and made additions thereto
and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare complete subsidiary ledgers for
all fund transfers for immediate tracking of the status of the fund at a given period;
(ii) continue to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer as well as the
Implementing Department to facilitate the immediate reconciliation of the
outstanding fund transfers from various Source Agencies and return the unused
funds from the results thereof, as warranted; (iii) in coordination with the
implementing departments to review and submit the noted deficiencies in item 1.12
of this Report; and (iv) evaluate the noted misstatements for appropriate adjusting
entry or reference to future similar transaction of the LGU, adhere with the
prescribed accounts as provided under COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 01,
2015;
(b) BAC to review the noted deficiencies in item 1.12 of this Report, henceforth,
see to it that all of the required documents as required by GPPB, are substantially
complied with and strictly observe the posting requirements as provided under the
RIRR of the RA No. 9184 and Annex “H” thereof; and
(c) in coordination with the implementing Department/s in case of future similar
undertakings, observe full adherence to the agreement or guidelines relative to the
specific fund held in trust.

9.30. We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations and made additional
recommendations that the Local Chief Executive to:
(a) in coordination with the MPDO and the LDC (i) continuously refer to the JMC
for appropriate guidance in crafting the Annual Investment Program and inclusion
of PPAs on the 20% DF that will aim to fully attain the desired socio-economic
development targets of the Municipality, with emphasis on the recent issuance on the
utilization of the fund, anchored with the LGUs long term and medium-term
development plan, introduce amendments if warranted to match the ever-evolving
needs of the public; and (ii) and MBO to review and conduct regular monitoring of
the fund’s appropriation/utilization in harmony with the approved CDP and AIP, if
warranted, for unimplemented PPAs that may not be pushed through in the
continuing appropriation, consider, the review/reallocation to other priority
development projects aligned with exiting plans during budget deliberations;
(b) request the Sangguniang Bayan, as agents in setting the direction of economic
and social development, and coordinating development efforts within its territorial
jurisdiction and will promote the general welfare of the locality and its inhabitants,
to conduct thorough review of the PPAs embodied in the proposed CDP and AIP that
are lucid and specific and are not contrary to law;
(c) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare justification and take note of
improper charges noted in item 1.12 of this Report to warrant the strict utilization of
the 20% Development Fund to only eligible expenditures and, ultimately, capitalize
on the Fund’s intended purpose. Refrain from charging these items or provide
allotment, appropriately under the 20% Development Fund; and
(d) direct the Municipal Accountant to check the noted deficiencies in item 1.14 of
this Memorandum and henceforth, see to it that the supporting documents of
projects/transactions are complete prior to making payment/s. To ensure
conformance with the processes and documentary requirements stated in COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and avert the recurrence thereof on future
similar undertakings.

10.48. We reiterated our last year’s recommendations and made additions thereto
and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require:
(a) the LDRRMO in coordination with the LDRRMC to (i) decide on the use of
unexpended balance of the fund and incorporate the same in the annually prepared
LDRRMFIP; (ii) see to it that all plans and programs embodied in the approved plan
and budget for disaster are fully implemented during the year by providing sufficient
appropriation for each PPAs and ensure the LDRRM activities require funding or
not; (iii) follow the PPAs embodied in the 3-year plan and the LDRRMFIP, otherwise,
the proposed changes should be accompanied with adequate recommendation and
approval of the LDRRMC and Sangguniang Bayan and LCE, respectively; (iv)
regularly review the 3-year LDDRMF plan to address current vulnerabilities and
account for changes, as warranted; and (iv) gradually comply with the minimum
standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources;
(b) the LDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Budget Officer to (i) initiate the reversion of funds to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund, lodged in the trust fund account existing formorethan5years; and (ii) along with the Municipal Treasurer facilitate actual transfer of
unexpended LDRRMF in the special trust fund as called for by the above-quoted
Circular;
(c) the Municipal Budget Officer in collaboration with the LDRMMO (i) review
notedinappropriatechargesinItem1.25ofthisReportandsubmitjustificationonthenotedcharges to the fund, for evaluation of the Audit Team; and (ii) henceforth,
avertmakingchargesnotrelatedtotheplanned PPAs to avoid possible sanction such as technical
malversation, for expenditures lacking appropriation.

11.20. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the implementing departments of the LGU to utilize in full the total
appropriated fund in accordance with the GPB approved by the DILG to ensure full
attainment of the GAD targets/objectives;
(b) direct the GAD Focal Point System and the GAD Focal Point Person as well
as the Implementing Departments (i) to monitor and strictly implement the GAD
PPAs in accordance with the DILG-approved GAD plan and budget; (ii) actively
monitor the effective implementation of the annual plan, introduce intervention as
necessary; (iii) require the concerned personnel involved to provide justification on
the noted ineligible charges and reported implementations to the fund as specified
under Items 1.15 and 1.17. of this Report; (iv) henceforth, submit the required
approved GPB and GAD AR within the reglementary period; and
(c) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant (as internal auditor in the absence of internal audit services) to review
the approved GAD Plan and Budget (i) strictly observe the required allocation of the
GAD fund in the approved budget that is directly related to the implementation of
GAD PPAs; (ii) charge the expenditures to their appropriate budget; and (iii) refrain
from charging ineligible expenditures to the fund.

12.37. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and made additions
therefrom that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) direct the HRMO to (i) continuously look for possible candidates from the
array of JO workers for permanency based on available vacant positions for
recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) to reduce the number of job
orders, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local
Government Code; (ii) propose the existing policy of hiring of JOs to the Sangguniang
Bayan inclusive of other necessitated requirements cited above; and (iii) prepare Job
Order contracts that reflect payment of JOs on a daily pay as called for by the above
quoted regulation;
(b) request from the Sangguniang Bayan, to give priority to the adoption of policy
in the hiring of JOs to provide a structured and objective selection of JOs based on the
necessity of the LGU;
(c) reduce the number of job orders in the Municipality by devising a
performance tool to objectively conduct work evaluation along with the HRMO and
immediate superiors of concerned JOs, to assess personnel who are value adding to
the LGU;
(d) in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and HRMO (i)
review/assesstheLGU’sorganizationalstructureand
staffingpatterninconsiderationwiththeservicerequirementas
well as financial capability of the LGU and consider recommending for the creation
of additional plantilla positions, if warranted, that may not be requiring eligibility
which may give chance to deserving JOs in the Municipality, in the next budgeting
period, to reduce number of required JOs to do essential services to the LGU; and (ii)
consider, if budget permits, the provision for a pay that is consistent with those
having the same positions and with salary similar to a comparable position in the
government; and (ii) ensure authorization of pay of JOs that is within the salary
grade equivalent for a comparable position, for consistency and objectivity in the
grant of salary; and
(e) direct the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to address the noted deficiencies
in Item 1.33 of this Report and ensure compliance or effect corrections needed, before
allowing payments thereof specifically on the requirements for DTR prior to the grant
of cash advances and the payment of wages to JOs based on actual days worked in
reference to the attached DTRs.

13.18. We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) look into the noted deficiencies and
lacking documents cited above and assist in the compliance thereof; and (ii)
henceforth, check the completeness of the supporting documents relative to
representation/s prior to payments.
(b) All concerned departments to (i) submit all the lacking documents identified
in item 1.6, and henceforth, see to it that all the required documents to support the
disbursements are submitted to the Audit Team; (ii) limit, if not totally eliminate, the
utilization of reimbursement and special purpose cash advance as a mode of payment
and pay all claims directly to the supplier as required under Section 93 of P.D. 1445;
(iv) consolidate all future purchases (planned events) for the year and employ
competitive bidding or SVP, as applicable, for meals and snacks served for
Programs/Projects/Activities so that the LGU can avail of the most advantageous
prices; and
(c) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to employ competitive bidding as the
primary mode of procurement unless the conditions are present for the use of most
appropriate procurement modality such as shopping and small value procurement.

We reiterated our previous and made additional recommendations and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the:

Bids and Awards Committee, its Secretariat, Technical Working Group and the General Services Officer to subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are present for the use of shopping and small value procurement; and

Vice-Mayor, SB Members, and all concerned departments to (i) submit all the lacking documents in Annex “D” and henceforth, see to it that all the required documents to support the disbursements are submitted to the Audit Team; (ii) properly plan PPAs that are to be implemented to avoid the occurrence of large discrepancies between the ordered snacks and meals and actual number of attendees; (iii) limit, if not totally eliminate, the utilization of reimbursement and special purpose cash advance as a mode of payment and pay all claims directly to the supplier as required under Section 93 of P.D. 1445; (iv) consolidate all future purchases (planned events) for the year and employ competitive bidding or SVP, as applicable, for meals and snacks served for Programs/Projects/Activities so that the LGU can avail of the most advantageous prices.

observations.managementAction:
Overage personnel were already relieved.

Similarly, the Audit Team was updated that there are 87 prepared termination letters for existing JOs with contracts ending June 30, 2023 are already prepared to be served as part of their compliance.

Finally, the HRMO issued a memorandum to each Department Head of the LGU for the determination of personnel recommended for termination as also part of their compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years recommendations and made additions thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
Municipal Accountant to (i) sustain the timely preparation of bank reconciliation statements; (ii) immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries reconciling items identified in the BRS prior the closing of the accounting books, if warranted; (iii) segregate the identified irreconcilable difference in the Cash in Bank -LCCA of the GF in the subsidiary ledgers to avoid further movement of balance; (iv) exert effort to reduce the unreconcilable balance; (v) observe standard practices in recording transactions by refraining from recording disbursements pertaining to succeeding year’s disbursements on current year records or vice versa; and (vi) verify the noted deviation on cancelled checks and if warranted, restore the CIB balance relative thereto;
the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of books of accounts at least quarterly to prevent further accumulation/occurrence of discrepancies in their respective records in the future; and
the Municipal Treasurer to assess/verify the identified reconciling items in the treasury’s books and if warranted, effect the necessary adjusting entry to correct the noted misstatements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
Sangguniang Bayan prioritized the adjustment of tax rates of the 15-year-old tax ordinance to increase the revenue generation of the LGU;
Municipal Treasurer to (i) exercise the power to examine the books of accounts of businesses, especially to those taxpayers with doubtful gross sales/tax declaration to ascertain the correctness of tax to be collected; and (ii) establish a stratified schedule to business tax of businesses who are unable to provide proof of gross receipts/sales to be embodied in the LGU’s local tax ordinance after undergoing public consultation and publication; and
BPLO and MITO to further improve the e-BOSS to increase the productivity, efficiency and effectiveness of the business permitting and licensing department of the LGU.

We reiterated our previous recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
resume in making representation with the PDIC regarding the options available, solutions for its settlement, or any relief from the large interest and penalties applied thereon; and
direct the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to continue to set aside funds for service debt in order to comply with the above-quoted regulations.

We reiterated our previous recommendations and made additions thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
Bids and Awards Committee, its Secretariat, Technical Working Group and the General Services Officer to subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are present for the use other modes of procurement;
All concerned departments to (i) submit all the lacking documents and henceforth, see to it that all the required documents to support the disbursements are submitted to the Audit Team; (ii) limit, if not totally eliminate, the utilization of reimbursement and special purpose cash advance as a mode of payment and pay all claims directly to the supplier as required under Section 93 of P.D. 1445; (iv) consolidate all future purchases (planned events) for the year and employ competitive bidding or SVP, as applicable, for meals and snacks served for Programs/Projects/Activities so that the LGU can avail of the most advantageous prices; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) look into the noted deficiencies and lacking documents cited above and assist in the compliance thereof; and (ii) henceforth, check the completeness of the supporting documents relative to representation/s prior to payments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the BAC and BAC TWG to (i) submit detailed explanation or valid justification on observations cited in Item 15.32 and 15.42 of this Memorandum; ii) in coordination with former BAC and BAC TWG composition, submit the deficient documents enumerated/identified in the discussions above (with emphasis in underline text), for the immediate evaluation of the Audit Team; and iii) henceforth, relentlessly improve the quality and extent of adherence with the requirements of RA 9184, its IRR and relevant rules and regulations in the Municipality’s particularly in the conduct of competitive bidding by noting all the observations/deviations conferred above and avoid the recurrence thereof in future similar undertakings;
attend to mandatory and continuing training/s on procurement and regularly checking for updates on procurement in the GPPB website, to keep abreast with the latest issuances and be able to comply with prescribed requirements accordingly; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to check and ensure the completeness of the documents and exercise prudence and be able to discern based on the documents submitted by the contractors are legitimate before effecting payment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to encourage the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continue and further enhance their compliance with respect to withholding and remitting premiums, contributions, and loan payments of GSIS, PagIbig, and PhilHealth which will ensure the personnel of the LGU receive their entitled benefits.
We encouraged Management to comply with the recommendations communicated through audit observation memoranda in order to prevent incurrence of audit suspensions, disallowances and charges.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

Municipal Accountant to (i) sustain the timely preparation of bank reconciliation statements; and (ii) immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries based from the results of the Municipal Treasurer’s investigation of the stale checks;

the Municipal Treasurer investigate the status of the noted stale checks; and

the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant (i) exhaust all efforts to secure all the necessary documents for the reconciliation of the long outstanding balance in the GF; and (ii) conduct reconciliation of books of accounts at least quarterly to prevent further accumulation/occurrence of discrepancies in the future.
observations.managementAction: The noted non-compliance on stale checks were already investigated by the Municipal Treasurer and were adjusted by the Accounting Office, accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations and made supplemental recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

make a representation with the PDIC regarding the options available, solutions for its settlement, or any relief from the large interest and penalties applied thereon; and

based on the result of the coordination, direct the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to set appropriation for service debt in order to comply with the above-quoted regulations.
observations.managementAction: Updates of the LGU’s compliance on this observation, as of this writing, was that the LCE had already communicated with the concerned agency as to the request for the condonation of interest and penalty charges coupled therein and will allot an appropriation for the amortization thereof on its annual budget after the results of the coordination.
The Management apprised the Audit Team thru a communication of the designation of special disbursing officers and related request for bonding thereof.

The LCE informed the Audit Team that plans for the regular usage of the Mobile Clinic is set to be facilitated this year (CY 2023).

On farm equipment’s received, the LCE added that all farmers of San Simon are made aware of the availability of the farm equipment anytime farmers need them and adequate monitoring of the borrowing farmers were told to be in place.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our last year’s recommendations and made additional recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

as the Chairman of the LDRRMC, ensure that the required composition of the Council be strictly adhered to in reference to Section 2, Rule 5 of the IRR of RA 10121;

direct the LDRRMO (i) together with the LDRRMC, to prepare and submit annually the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program of the LGU in reference to COA Circular 2012-002; (ii) see to it that all plans and programs contained in the approved plan and budget for disaster are fully implemented during the year; (iii) regularly review the 3-year LDDRMF plan to address current vulnerabilities and account for changes, as warranted; and (iii) coordinate with the MSWD on the gradual compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and

in coordination with the LDRRMO and MSWDO, instruct the General Services Officer to prepare the required reports pertaining to in-kind donations to the LGU and submit the same within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
The Management informed the Audit Team of the monthly Submission of Report of Sources and Utilization, preparation of the LDRRMFIP will also be facilitated and agreed to coordinate all donations with the GSO for future similar donations in kind received by the LGU.

Similarly, the required membership of the LDRRMC was already complied by the LGU as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We restated our prior recommendation and made additional recommendations thereof that the Local Chief Executive:

instruct the BAC to include in the items of work the related expenditures pertaining to its compliance with the general guidelines on the posting of tarpaulin with the required size and format as mentioned in the Circular, as soon as the award has been made;

direct the Municipal Accountant to require the compliance of the herein requirements prior to payment of expenditures; and

for continued failure of non-compliance with the required posting recommend disciplinary actions to all erring employee/personnel, where appropriate, in reference to Section 127 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Audit Team was apprised of their compliance with the above cited observation starting CY 2023.

The Municipal Engineer has already inspected the area and made rectifications on the audit observation as to the deficiencies on the temporary facility.

During the exit conference, the LGU is targeting to transfer with the permanent MRF of the LGU within the 1st semester of CY 2023.

As to volume reduction of waste, the MENRO informed the Team that aside from Holcim the LGU had also negotiated with Republic Cement on its collection of plastic waste as a way to reduce cost for the payment of final disposal of waste with Metro Clark.

The LGU has three (3) existing garbage truck were the LCE targets to purchase additional garbage truck to cater all the 14 Barangays of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated and supplement our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

in close coordination with the Treasurers’ Office and Accounting Department, (i) review the variance noted in the Due to GSIS account as discussed in item 16.14 of this Memorandum, to facilitate necessary adjustments; and (ii) make sure that the same amount withheld is remitted the following month; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjustments of the liabilities and equity accounts from the errors found during prior year’s audit to correct the balances of these accounts and cause the remittance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management informed the Audit Team that errors noted were corrected/adjusted accordingly, this year (CY 2023).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) sustain the timely preparation of bank
reconciliation statements; and (ii) immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries
based from the results of the Municipal Treasurer’s investigation of the stale checks;
(b) the Municipal Treasurer investigate the status of the noted stale checks; and
(c) the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant (i) exhaust all efforts
to secure all the necessary documents for the reconciliation of the long outstanding
balance in the GF; and (ii) conduct reconciliation of books of accounts at least
quarterly to prevent further accumulation/occurrence of discrepancies in the future.

We reiterated and supplemented our previous recommendations and
management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) reconstitute an Inventory Committee, including at least one member each
from the Accounting and Property Department of the LGU in pursuance to item 5.2.
of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 who will spearhead the conduct of
complete physical count of all the LGU’s properties, taking into account the
provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs;
(b) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to exert
efforts to prepare the Property Cards and Property, Plant, and Equipment Ledger
Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’
acquisitions and aiming for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are
covered;
(c) require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to (i) work coordinately with the
assistance of the Municipal Engineer on the inventory of all local road projects with
complete description and cost segregation of road components to ensure proper
recording and control of the assets and prepare the respective Local Road Network
Ledger Cards and Local Road Network Property Card; and (ii) cause the tracing of
the initial recognition of all PPE to comply with the preparation and maintenance of
Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards;
(d) enjoin Accountant, GSO, and Municipal Engineer to attend training/s relative
to LRAMM by the DILG, adopt and follow the procedures set forth therein and
comply with the requirements thereof on reporting as prescribed under COA
Circular 2015-008; and
(e) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the appropriate adjusting

1.23. We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Executive:
(a) reconstitute an Inventory Committee to include each Department Head to
facilitate inventory-taking by office of all properties of the Municipality and to submit
the Report of the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE);
(b) submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year;
(c) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) Set
out the maintenance of Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’
acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered;
and (ii) reconcile their records with the inventory results;
(d) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) record in the books of accounts the
completed road network for CY 2018-2019 and the unrecorded real properties of the

value of PPE in the financial statements; and (ii) improve the accounting and
recording of properties and provide registries and subsidiary records so that relevant
details are available to Management and stakeholders; and

accounting of the Municipality’s PPE and for the fair presentation of the asset
accounts in the financial statements.

2.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) adopt the perpetual inventory method and
recognize inventories as asset at the time of purchase, and expense based on the
monthly issuance reports; (ii) maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of

the balances of the inventory records with the records of the GSO; and

timely the corresponding control documents such as Request Issuance Slip (RIS) and
Stock Cards to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt
and issuance of supplies and materials; and (ii) conduct semestral physical count of
inventories and submit the RCPI within the prescribed deadline.

penalties agreed upon; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
involve the General Services Officer in the processing of purchase requests and in the keeping of records to keep track of the movement of stocks on hand from receipt until issuance for accounting, control and accountability purposes.
initiate efforts towards the provision of  storage so that the procurement of  supplies  in significant quantity can be stored to lessen the administrative cost in frequent procurements and reimbursements.
require the General Services Officer to (i)  henceforth, prepare and submit documents on purchases of the Municipality; (ii) prepare and maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies; (iii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (iv) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and
require the Acting Municipal Accountant to recognize all purchase of supplies and materials for stock under the proper inventory account; prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as provided by the above quoted regulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) seek legal counsel from the Provincial Legal Officer for the Municipality’s proper guidance for the necessary  courses of action in response to the demand letter from the PDIC; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the financial liabilities of the Municipality including interest and penalties relative to its loan and henceforth, perform a periodic reconciliation of balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic with respective lending banks/institutions to reflect accurate year-end balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the General Services Officer instruct all drivers concerned to prepare and submit properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket, Monthly Report of Official Travel sand Report of Fuel Consumption and other required procedures in adherence to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate and cooperate closely with each other for the immediate submission of the December 2017 disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents including monthly financial statements and supporting schedules and documents; and (b) improve compliance in the submission of accounts and financial reports within the prescribed time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive
instruct end users in the next budgeting period to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year;
from the submitted PPMPs require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP); and
cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to furnish COA copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5)  days  after  the  perfection  of the contract and  sending of notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to  COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; (b) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and (c) improve compliance in proposing projects that are capital investments in nature with long-term benefits to the beneficiaries/constituents.

We recommended that the Management sustain its efforts in making use of the comprehensive land use planning (CLUP) for a disaster-resilient community and in complying with most of the minimum standards in disaster preparedness as required by the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, among others.
However, for the Municipality to be considered fully disaster-prepared, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Designated LDRRM Officer to include in its plan, the compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; (b)  Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the fund to the Special Trust Fund with actual cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to   in the books the said transfer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a)  initiate efforts to scout for a permanent site for the establishment of an MRF; (b) if donation is not possible, seek financial assistance from the Provincial Government of Pampanga if the Municipality’s budget could not afford the purchase of lot for the MRF; (c) initiate efforts in the provision of budget for the construction of an MRF; and (d) make follow up with the concerned government offices for the approval of the Municipality’s ten-year SWMP so that programs thereon may be incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality; (e) instruct the General Services Officer to take action to protect the equipment from unnecessary open exposure to the elements of nature.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LCPC/BCPC Members to prepare a separate LCPC budget with clearly identified PPAs even if such are not separately funded but merely attributed in case of the Municipality’s inability to allocate the required 1% from its IRA to facilitate performance evaluation and compliance with RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider hiring additional accounting staff, if the budget permits, so the Accounting Office can (a) maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund; and (b) prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will serve as tools in informing Management in evaluating the viability of the economic enterprise operated by the Municipality.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person and its TWG improve compliance to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 in the proper and systematic manner of attributing expenses to GAD such as clear statement on the gender issue being addressed; and ensure/monitor that the duly approved programs, projects and activities as stated in the approved annual GAD Plan are the ones to be implemented accordingly.

We commended the Municipality for its efforts in complying with the requirements of the rules and regulations and we recommended that it sustain its efforts to being compliant to regulations on the pertinent BIR, GSIS and HDMF regulations. However, the Due to BIR balance of ₱4,277,292.81 should be accounted by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive request the BAC, BAC-TWG to (a) continue to sustain efforts in complying with the prescribed procurement procedures under the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184; and (b) comply with the submission to the Audit Team for its further evaluation of the lacking documents to support the payments and henceforth, adhere to the prescribed procedures under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and other pertinent regulations in the documentation of claims.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the implementing offices, comply with the submission of the Liquidation Report and supporting DVs of all completed projects not yet submitted for post audit.

maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary; and
henceforth, comply with the required documentation for similar transactions as mentioned above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials (a) to submit immediately to the Audit Team the required documents as discussed above for further evaluation and subsequent audit action; and (b) henceforth, strictly comply with the regulation and due dates on the rendition of accounts and reports.

15.6   	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Budget Officer and Accountant to discontinue payment of membership dues of league/federation of appointed officials outside of those recognized by the LGC; and (b) require the refund of the same from the recipients thereof.

16.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) henceforth, ensure compliance with Section 335 on the prohibition of incurring expenditures for religious purpose; and (b) direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to (i) exercise due diligence in the  certification of expenditures; (ii) ensure the completeness of the required documentary requirements to support the disposition of funds thereof; and (iii) submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team for further evaluation and subsequent audit action.

We recommended that management (a) require the LCPC/BCPC Members to appropriate the 1% of IRA to LCPC programs, projects and activities as stipulated in accordance with Section 15 of RA9344 and  DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 notwithstanding the appropriated budget for GAD and (b) properly identify relevant programs, projects and activities in order to strengthen and promote the children’s welfare.

18.5	We recommended that the Management (a) seek legal counsel from the Provincial Legal Officer preparatory to the impending foreclosure in order for the Municipality to take the necessary legal courses of action; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to perform a periodic reconciliation of balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic with the respective lending banks/institutions to reflect accurate year-end balances.

19.4    We commended Management for its consistency in remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291, however, we recommend the verification if there were over remittance of contributions since the account has a negative balance.

20.6   We recommended that Management continue to withhold taxes and ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007 and the verification of the large amount of ending balance of the account carried over from previous years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all persons deemed responsible to settle all suspended transactions within the prescribed period to prevent them from maturing into disallowances.

We recommended that management: (a) discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the Local Chief Executive other than those specified under COA Circular No. 97-002; (b) cause the immediate settlement/liquidation of outstanding cash advances or refund of the excess cash advances  pursuant to the said COA circular; (c) allow the grant of cash advances to duly designated disbursing officer for time-bound events or special undertaking at the amount deemed reasonable based on realistic estimate.
observations.reason: There were still cash advances granted to the Local Chief Executive in the first semester of CY 2016. It was only discontinued in the second half of the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned drivers and agency personnel to prepare Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Report of Fuel Consumption, and submit the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable, effectively controlled and properly accounted.
observations.reason: The Management just complied with the audit recommendations during the last quarter of the year. Compliance is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity on the PPAs of the Government Agencies to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people and to enable the Audit Team to comply with the submission of the Quarterly Report of the PPAs to the Regional Director for monitoring compliance.
observations.reason: Not all of the LGU’s infrastructure projects have  billboards, though procurement results and processes were posted in the bulletin board and in three conspicuous places within the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) reconstitute an inventory committee to include each Department Head to facilitate inventory taking by Office of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality; b) require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances; (c) direct the General Services Officer to prepare Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for all PPE items, starting with the current and recent years’ acquisition until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at least every three years.
observations.reason:
The Management has exerted all the efforts to verify the balance but failed to identify further due to the following reasons:

No property records were maintained in previous years.
No regular physical inventory of PPE was conducted.
Only recent acquisitions of properties/ equipment were covered by ARE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) and maintain Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) on the issuance of property and semi-expendable items at least every three years or whenever there is transfer of accountability.
observations.reason: Only recent acquisitions of properties/ equipment were covered by ARE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate efforts with the barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003;  (b) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; (c) enforce the Sangguniang Bayan ordinance on the “no segregation, no collection” policy; and (d) initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials.
observations.reason: The required ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan was just recently approved by the Saggunian and submitted for review to the Environmental Management Bureau.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council propose programs and projects that contribute to development and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure pursuant to Section 3 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to provide long-term benefits to the constituents.
observations.reason: There were programs and projects in the AIP of the Municipality that do not contribute to development and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Budget Officer maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO);  (b) Municipal Accountant submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF every 15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor as required in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (c) LDDRMF Council consider proposing projects for disaster preparedness not only on consumables but also on the acquisition of life-saving equipment.
observations.reason:
Still no RAAO was maintained by the Budget Officer. 

No Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipality procure its common-use supplies directly from the Procurement Service in compliance with Section 1 of A.O. No. 17, s. 2011 and Section 21 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act FY 2015.
observations.reason: Not all of the procurement of supplies were procured in the DBM-PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) the members of the BAC (i) subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are present for the use of shopping and small value procurement as alternative mode of procurement; (ii) comply strictly with Section 54.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the prohibition of splitting of contract, and (b) direct all offices concerned and the BAC Secretariat to prepare a complete Annual Procurement Plan containing all the requirements of the Municipality, procurement schedule and mode of procurement.
observations.reason:
Procurement of the Municipality was mostly through reimbursement and procured through shopping, thus, was not subjected to bidding.

No complete APP was submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management establish policies and control procedures to ensure that (a) acknowledgement by the recipient is properly/completely documented on the approved cash assistance, bearing his name, signature and the amount received; (b) each  person or individual seeking assistance present valid identification to prove identity or  Certificate of Indigency as issued by the Punong Barangays where he/she resides; and (c) the grant of aid to private and religious institutions is stopped in pursuance of Article 454 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: There were still donations made by the Municipality and given to private and religious organizations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) submit the lacking documents supporting the subject disbursements in accordance with RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; (b) submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies in the supporting documents; and (c) require the General Services Officer to submit within five (5) working days to the Office of the Auditor copies of purchase orders, and notification of the date and time of deliveries as prescribed in COA Circular Nos 2009-001 and 92-386.
observations.reason: Many disbursement vouchers were still not supported with complete documentations. However, compliance of recommendation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) approving and reviewing officials concerned on disbursements do not allow the frequent reimbursement of meals and snacks deemed unnecessary or could be dispensed with and; (b) officials concerned minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks during ordinary staff meetings or conferences to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.
observations.reason: There were still frequent reimbursements of meals allowed and charged to representations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) officials concerned strictly comply with the regulation on the documentation of payments to establish the validity and propriety of disbursements; and (b) the Municipal Accountant carefully examine all DVs to ensure that payments are made only on duly approved vouchers supported with complete and properly accomplished documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.reason: Many disbursement vouchers were still not supported with complete documentations. However, compliance of recommendation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer  facilitate the submission of verified daily time record of regular officers and employees for CY 2015 in accordance with CSC rules to support/substantiate the payment of salaries, and ensure that printed name of immediate supervisor is affixed below his/her signature to establish accountability and responsibility.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer require the personnel under the “job order” status to submit duly verified/accepted accomplishment report every month before paying their salaries.
observations.reason: Not all of the personnel under “job order” status submitted duly verified/accepted accomplishment report every month and attached to the payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management set criteria and issue specific guidelines in the grant of scholarship and to strictly comply with the regulation on documentation of payments to protect the interest of the government and to prevent unnecessary and irregular disbursements and to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.reason: Documentation of the said scholarship grant is on-going.
